name: Oracle MICROS Simphony Restaurant POS API
what_it_does: Oracle MICROS Simphony cloud restaurant POS REST API for enterprise restaurants, hotel food and beverage, quick service, and casino food service to manage orders, menus, and transaction data. Enables AI agents to manage menu item and price configuration for restaurant menu management automation, handle order creation and table service management for restaurant ordering integration, access transaction and sales data for restaurant analytics automation, retrieve labor and time-punch data for restaurant workforce management automation, manage loyalty program integration via Oracle OPERA and Simphony for hospitality loyalty automation, handle kitchen display system routing for restaurant order routing automation, access inventory depletion and menu 86-management for restaurant inventory automation, retrieve check and payment data for restaurant revenue management automation, manage multi-location configuration for enterprise restaurant chain management, and integrate Oracle Simphony with OPERA hotel PMS, Oracle Cloud ERP, and hospitality ecosystem for end-to-end enterprise food and beverage management.
use_cases: Integrating online orders from enterprise restaurant platform agents
Syncing menus from Oracle hotel F&B management agents
Reporting sales from casino food service analytics agents
Managing labor from enterprise restaurant workforce agents
not_for: Independent single-location restaurants under 10 terminals (use Square or Toast)
Retail point-of-sale outside hospitality (use Oracle NetSuite POS)
best_when: An enterprise restaurant chain, hotel food and beverage operation, casino food service, or quick service brand wanting AI agents to automate menu management, order integration, sales analytics, and labor management within Oracle Simphony's enterprise POS platform.
avoid_when: ORACLE SIMPHONY DEVELOPER PROGRAM CERTIFICATION REQUIRED: Oracle Simphony API access for third-party integrations requires Oracle Hospitality Integration Platform (OHIP) partner certification; automated integration deployment without OHIP certification creates Oracle partner agreement violation and may be blocked at API gateway level. SIMPHONY VERSION COMPATIBILITY FOR ENTERPRISE CHAINS: Oracle Simphony has multiple active major versions (Simphony 1, Simphony 2, Simphony Essential); enterprise restaurant chains may operate mixed Simphony versions across locations; automated integrations must verify Simphony version per property before order injection; cross-version API behavior differences create integration failures at specific locations. PCI-DSS PAYMENT CARD DATA ISOLATION: Simphony API access for order and transaction data must not expose payment card data; Oracle Simphony tokenizes card data at terminal level; automated analytics integrations must use tokenized transaction references only; direct card number access via API creates PCI-DSS scope expansion.

auth.notes: Oracle MICROS Simphony uses OAuth 2.0 through Oracle Hospitality Integration Platform (OHIP). REST API with JSON. Austin, Texas HQ (Oracle Corporation, NYSE: ORCL). Oracle acquired MICROS Systems in 2014 for $5.3B. Simphony is Oracle's cloud POS for hospitality. Enterprise restaurant chains, hotel F&B, casinos, stadiums. Integrates with Oracle OPERA PMS, Oracle Cloud ERP, Oracle Loyalty. Competes with NCR Aloha, Lightspeed Restaurant, and Revel for enterprise restaurant POS.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise subscription per terminal per month
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-terminal monthly subscription; enterprise negotiated pricing for chains; Oracle Hospitality partner program fees for integrators; OHIP API usage fees
pricing.notes: Austin TX. Oracle Corporation (NYSE: ORCL). Enterprise per-terminal subscription. Oracle Hospitality partner program required for API integrations. OHIP API usage fees.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OHIP OAuth client; order and menu queries throttled per property; webhooks for order and transaction events via OHIP

known_agent_gotchas: OHIP PARTNER CERTIFICATION MANDATORY FOR API ACCESS: Oracle Hospitality Integration Platform (OHIP) requires partner certification for third-party API integrations with Simphony; automated integrations cannot access Simphony API without completing OHIP partner program certification; certification process includes Oracle security review and integration testing; factor OHIP certification timeline (weeks to months) into integration project planning

SIMPHONY VERSION COMPATIBILITY MATRIX: Enterprise restaurant chains running multi-location Simphony deployments may have different Simphony versions at different properties; Simphony 1 and Simphony 2 have different API surfaces via OHIP; automated order injection must verify property's Simphony version before API call pattern; single-version automation deployed to mixed-version estate creates property-specific failures

PROPERTY-LEVEL API CONFIGURATION REQUIRED: Oracle Simphony OHIP integration requires property-level configuration of integration modules; automated deployment to new restaurant properties requires OHIP module activation per property; automated order injection without property-level OHIP configuration creates API authentication failure for unconfigured properties

KITCHEN DISPLAY ROUTING CONFIGURATION DEPENDENCY: Automated order injection into Oracle Simphony routes to kitchen displays based on menu item revenue center and order type configuration; automated order injection with incorrect revenue center mapping routes orders to wrong kitchen station; validate revenue center and KDS routing configuration per property before automated order injection

ORACLE CLOUD DEPENDENCY FOR ENTERPRISE FEATURES: Oracle Simphony enterprise features (enterprise menu management, cross-property analytics) require Oracle Cloud infrastructure; automated enterprise-scale workflows depend on Oracle Cloud availability in addition to Simphony; Oracle Cloud maintenance windows may affect automated workflows beyond standard Simphony downtime

TRANSACTION DATA PCI-DSS SCOPE MANAGEMENT: Oracle Simphony OHIP transaction data API returns tokenized payment references; automated analytics workflows must use transaction tokens, not card data; any custom OHIP integration that attempts to capture card data expands PCI-DSS scope to include the integration system; design analytics pipelines to use only tokenized transaction identifiers
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise restaurant POS. PCI-DSS, SOC2. OAuth2 via OHIP. US/EU/UK/AU. Transaction data, employee records, and food service operations.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise restaurant POS. PCI-DSS, SOC2. OAuth2. Global. Transaction, employee, and food service data.
